divergence_notes: HOSTED-vs-HOSTED structural disagreement: jayyhk's erdos_401 states the text's question directly (`exists f : N -> R, Tendsto f atTop atTop && forall r >= 1, ...`); plby has no single final statement matching the text -- its theorem_1 proves the property for one explicit witness omega(r) = (gamma/16)(p_{r+1}-1)/log p_{r+1} without the exists-f/Tendsto wrapper (the wrapper follows since omega -> infinity, which jayyhk proves separately). Drafted statement follows the text (and jayyhk's shape).

a1, a2 required positive (`0 < a1 && 0 < a2`) as in both hosted theorems; the text says only 'there exist a_1,a_2'. Positivity is the standard ErGr80 positive-integer convention and strengthens the statement (0! = 1 would otherwise admit degenerate witnesses such as a2 = 0).
r restricted to `1 <= r` as in both hosted theorems; the text presupposes p_r exists (the product 2^n 3^n ... p_r^n), so r >= 1 is implicit; for r = 0 the product would be empty.

2^n 3^n ... p_r^n formalized inline as `(prod i in Finset.range r, Nat.nth Nat.Prime i)^n`; both hosted define P r = prod_{j in range r} p(j+1) with p(j) = Nat.nth Nat.Prime (j-1) -- identical value (Nat.nth Nat.Prime 0 = 2, ..., Nat.nth Nat.Prime (r-1) = p_r). No new definition introduced.

f typed N -> R (matches jayyhk); the text does not specify a codomain, and f(r) multiplies log n, so real values are the natural reading. The inequality a1 + a2 > n + f(r) log n is stated in R with the standard casts, exactly as in both hosted theorems.
'for infinitely many n' rendered as Set.Infinite of the witness set, matching both hosted theorems.
Statement form: FC house style `answer(True) <-> ...` (solved yes/no question); jayyhk states the bare proposition, plby only the omega-instance.
